title: Date-Nut Energy Bites
description: No-bake sweet bites of dates, peanuts, and seeds. Perfect travel fuel and dessert-level craving fix.
explanation: These are the plant-based answer to packaged energy bars: sticky dates bind nuts and seeds into bite-sized snacks that travel well without refrigeration for a day.
Use soft dates; if dry, soak 10 minutes in warm water and drain well before blending.
ingredients: 1–2 tsp Water only if needed to bind
instructions: Pulse peanuts and seeds in a mixer to a coarse meal—not a fine flour.
Add dates, cocoa, cardamom, and salt. Blend to a sticky dough.
If too dry, add water a few drops at a time.
Roll into 10–12 small balls. Optional: roll in sesame or cocoa.
Chill 20 minutes to firm up, then store in a jar.
stepNotes: A sticky dough that holds when pressed is the goal.
Oily peanut butter texture means you over-blended—pulse in shorter bursts.
For travel, wrap individually in parchment.
Refrigerate up to a week; freeze up to a month.
Cocoa makes them taste like chocolate truffles.

variationSections.title: Mix-in ideas
items: Oats for a chewier bar texture (press into a pan and cut).
Espresso powder for a coffee kick.

variationSections.title: Allergy swaps
items: Use roasted chana or sunflower seeds instead of peanuts.
Skip cocoa if you want a pure date-cardamom taste.
Add a scoop of plant protein for higher-protein bites (may need extra date).
